summary: postinstall script harvests AWS credentials and exfiltrates to hardcoded remote IP
details: The package's postinstall.js executes automatically on `npm install` and performs cloud-credential theft against the installer's environment. It shells out to dump `AWS_*` environment variables, queries EC2 instance metadata service (http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/) for IAM role credentials, and calls STS / Secrets Manager / SSM / S3 across regions. The collected output is base64-encoded inside a YAML cover-story (keys `ecto_module`, `ship_deck`, `cargo_hold`) and HTTP-PUT to hardcoded targets at `http://127.0.0.1:32271` and `http://154.57.164.64:32271` on paths `/api/modules/ECT-839201` and `/api/modules/ECT-472839`, with `os.hostname()` included for victim correlation. Execution is gated to evade scanners: the script returns early when `GITHUB_ACTIONS`, `RUNNER_OS`, or `CI` env vars are set, when cwd is `/tmp`, or when the hostname does not match a 12-hex-character pattern; it only fires inside `/app`. The combination of IMDS credential harvest, plaintext exfiltration to a hardcoded non-publisher IP, and explicit sandbox-evasion gating is unambiguous supply-chain attack behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
